title: Moroccan Seven-Vegetable Couscous

ingredients.name: couscous (fine semolina / sooji)
visual_checks: Fine sooji (rava) is the best Indian substitute for couscous
ingredients.notes: Fine sooji (rava) is the best Indian substitute for couscous

subs.name: Ready-made couscous from a specialty store, prepared as per packet instructions; or use quinoa for a gluten-free version

ingredients.name: pumpkin or butternut squash, 3 cm cubes
visual_checks: Lauki (bottle gourd) is a good budget substitute
ingredients.notes: Lauki (bottle gourd) is a good budget substitute

subs.name: Sweet potato or diced raw banana (kaccha kela) for a denser, starchier stew
Ridge gourd (turai) or regular zucchini; add in the last 8 minutes as they cook faster
White rajma (kidney beans) or boiled chana dal; both hold their shape in the broth

steps.text: Prepare the couscous first so it is ready when the stew is done. Bring 300 ml water to a boil and take off the heat. Pour over the rava (couscous) in a bowl, add 0.5 tsp salt and 1 tsp oil, stir once, then cover tightly with a plate. Set aside for 5 minutes.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium flame. Add the onion wedges and cook for 5 minutes until they soften and turn translucent at the edges without browning.
Add carrot and pumpkin to the pot. Stir in cumin, coriander powder, turmeric, cinnamon and red chilli powder; cook for 2 minutes until the spices coat the vegetables and smell fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and 400 ml water. Season generously with salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a low simmer, cover and cook for 15 minutes until the carrots are mostly tender.
Add the bottle gourd (or zucchini) and chickpeas. Simmer uncovered for 8 minutes — the gourd should be just tender, the broth fragrant and slightly thickened. Taste and adjust salt.

Fluff the rested couscous (rava) with a fork — it should be light and separated, not clumped. Mound the couscous in a large shallow bowl or individual plates, ladle the hot vegetable broth and vegetables over and around, and finish with fresh coriander leaves.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rava → Ready-made couscous from a specialty store, prepared as per packet instructions; or use quinoa for a gluten-free version; pumpkin → Sweet potato or diced raw banana (kaccha kela) for a denser, starchier stew; chickpea → White rajma (kidney beans) or boiled chana dal; both hold their shape in the broth; bottle-gourd → Ridge gourd (turai) or regular zucchini; add in the last 8 minutes as they cook faster
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Fine sooji (rava / upma rava) is a nearly identical substitute for couscous — the same grain, the same absorption texture. The vegetable stew cooks in any kadai or pressure cooker. All spices are from a standard Indian pantry. Budget-friendly and naturally vegan, this dish needs no specialist equipment or imports.
